steps.title: Simplify the left side
steps.content:
The left side $\frac{14x}{7y}$ simplifies to $\frac{2x}{y}$, so the equation becomes: $$\frac{2x}{y} = 2\sqrt{w+19}$$
Divide both sides by 2 to isolate the square root: $$\frac{x}{y} = \sqrt{w+19}$$

steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content: To eliminate the square root, square both sides: $$\left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2 = w + 19$$

steps.title: Isolate w
steps.content:
Subtract 19 from both sides: $$w = \left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2 - 19$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
